zh.Bucklow, Cheshire, England: 英國爾巴可洛福 希爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Isabel de Massey於1238年出生在英國爾巴可洛福 希爾時，她的父親Hamon de Massey V43歲，母親Alice de Whitney32歲。她於大約1278年與Sir Ralph de Baguley在英國英格蘭爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世在英國爾阿爾特林哈姆。

zh-hans.Bucklow, Cheshire, England: 英国爾巴可洛福 希爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Isabel de Massey于1238年出生在英国爾巴可洛福 希爾时，她的父亲Hamon de Massey V43岁，母亲Alice de Whitney32岁。她于大约1278年与Sir Ralph de Baguley在英国英格兰爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世在英国爾阿爾特林哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Masey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Isabel de Massey was born in 1238, in Bucklow Hill, Cheshire, England, her father, Hamon de Massey V, was 43 and her mother, Alice de Whitney, was 32. She married Sir Ralph de Baguley about 1278, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in Altrincham, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Isabel de Massey est née en 1238, à Bucklow Hill, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Hamon de Massey V, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Alice de Whitney, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Ralph de Baguley environ 1278, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée à Altrincham, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Isabel de Massey 1238 in Bucklow Hill, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hamon de Massey V 43 und ihre Mutter Alice de Whitney 32. Sie hat um 1278 in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Ralph de Baguley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb in Altrincham, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabel de Massey è nata il nel 1238 nel Bucklow Hill, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Hamon de Massey V, aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Alice de Whitney, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Sir Ralph de Baguley circa 1278, nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel Altrincham, Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel de Masseyが1238年にイギリスチェシュアイアブックロウ ヒルで生まれた時，父Hamon de Massey Vは43歳，母Alice de Whitneyは32歳であった。約1278年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアでSir Ralph de Baguleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスチェシュアイアアルトリンチャムで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel de Massey(이)가 1238년 영국 체실르 벅로 힐에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hamon de Massey V(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Whitney(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 대략 1278년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 Sir Ralph de Baguley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 체실르 알트린챔에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabel de Massey nasceu em 1238, em Bucklow Hill, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hamon de Massey V, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Whitney, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Ralph de Baguley aproximadamente 1278, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu em Altrincham, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Isabel de Massey родилась в 1238 г. в местности букклоу хилл, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Hamon de Massey V было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Alice de Whitney, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Ralph de Baguley около 1278 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла в местности алтринчам, чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Isabel de Massey nació en 1238, en Bucklow Hill, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Hamon de Massey V, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Alice de Whitney, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Sir Ralph de Baguley aproximadamente 1278, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en Altrincham, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
